_comment: Staging file for Claude-in-session AI summaries. See pipeline/enrichers/prompts.py for the contract. Run `python -m pipeline.enrichers.summarize` to stamp these onto district profiles. Regenerated 2026-05-17 for all 42 districts after the spedps charter-aggregate fix.

37-67967.overview: Alpine Union Elementary is a small elementary (K-6) district in eastern San Diego County. About 242 of its students have an Individualized Education Program (IEP), and 48 of them — roughly 20% — have an autism (ASD) eligibility. Around 19% of those autistic students spend most of the school day, 80% or more, in a general-education classroom.
37-67967.what_this_means_for_parents: In this district about 19% of autistic students are in a general-education classroom for most of the day, so most spend a meaningful share of the day in specialized settings, and about 2% are in a fully separate setting. Across all students with disabilities — a broader group that includes autism along with many other categories — 56% are in general-education classrooms most of the day. On 2024-25 state tests, students with disabilities scored about 99 points below standard in English and 106 points below in math, while students overall were 9 and 23 points below, leaving a wide gap between the two groups. Students with disabilities were chronically absent at about 30% (compared with 23% for all students) and suspended at about 4% (compared with 3%).

37-67983.overview: Borrego Springs Unified is a rural K-12 district in the eastern desert of San Diego County. Its full reported special-education population is 667 students with an IEP, of whom 23 — about 3% — have an autism (ASD) eligibility. Roughly 91% of those autistic students spend most of the school day in a general-education classroom.
37-67983.what_this_means_for_parents: About 91% of autistic students here are in a general-education classroom for most of the day, and none are reported in a fully separate setting. Among all students with disabilities, a broader group than autism alone, 98% are in general-education classrooms most of the day. On 2024-25 state tests, students with disabilities scored about 132 points below standard in English and 182 points below in math, compared with 47 and 85 points below for students overall. Students with disabilities were chronically absent at about 25%, close to the 24% rate for all students, and were suspended at about 3%.

37-68007.overview: Cardiff Elementary is a very small elementary (K-6) district on the North Coast of San Diego County. On 2024-25 state tests, students overall scored above standard — 54 points above in English and 40 above in math — while students with disabilities scored 14 points below in English and 23 below in math. No students were suspended in 2024-25 in either group, and chronic absenteeism was about 10% for students with disabilities and 7% for all students.

37-68171.overview: Julian Union High is a very small high school district in eastern San Diego County, with about 22 students who have an IEP. The number of autistic students is small enough that the state does not report it separately, so this profile describes all students with disabilities rather than autism specifically. About 68% of students with disabilities spend most of the school day in a general-education classroom.
37-68171.what_this_means_for_parents: Because this is a very small district, the state withholds autism-specific counts and several other measures to protect student privacy, so the figures here cover all students with disabilities together. About 68% of students with disabilities are in a general-education classroom for most of the school day. State distance-from-standard test scores were not separately reported for students with disabilities in a district this size. The suspension rate was about 11% for both students with disabilities and the student body overall.

On 2024-25 state tests, students with disabilities scored about 102 points below standard in English and 160 below in math, while students overall were 1 and 63 points below. Students with disabilities were chronically absent at about 26% (compared with 19% for all students) and suspended at about 7% (compared with 4%).

37-68437.overview: Vallecitos Elementary is a very small elementary (K-6) district in eastern San Diego County, serving about 32 students with an IEP. The number of autistic students is small enough that the state does not report it separately, so this profile describes all students with disabilities rather than autism specifically. About 78% of students with disabilities spend most of the school day in a general-education classroom.
37-68437.what_this_means_for_parents: Autism-specific counts are not separately reported for a district this small, so the figures here describe all students with disabilities together. About 78% are in a general-education classroom for most of the school day. On 2024-25 state tests, students with disabilities scored about 63 points below standard in English and 112 below in math, compared with 1 and 54 points below for students overall. Students with disabilities were chronically absent at about 3% — lower than the 9% rate for all students — and were suspended at about 5%.

37-68452.overview: Vista Unified is a large K-12 district in the North Inland region of San Diego County. About 4,138 of its students have an IEP, and 735 of them — roughly 18% — have an autism (ASD) eligibility. Around 33% of those autistic students spend most of the school day in a general-education classroom.
37-68452.what_this_means_for_parents: About 33% of autistic students in this district are in a general-education classroom for most of the day, while about 5% are in a fully separate setting, so many spend part of the day in specialized settings. Among all students with disabilities, a broader group than autism alone, 66% are in general-education classrooms most of the day. On 2024-25 state tests, students with disabilities scored about 86 points below standard in English and 114 below in math, while students overall were 17 and 49 points below. Students with disabilities were chronically absent at about 23% (compared with 18% for all students) and suspended at about 6% (compared with 3%).

37-73551.overview: Carlsbad Unified is a large K-12 district on the North Coast of San Diego County. 37-73551.what_this_means_for_parents: About 35% of autistic students in this district are in a general-education classroom for most of the day, while about 4% are in a fully separate setting, so many spend part of the day in specialized settings. Among all students with disabilities, a broader group than autism alone, 72% are in general-education classrooms most of the day. On 2024-25 state tests, students overall scored above standard — 48 points above in English and 21 above in math — while students with disabilities scored 47 points below in English and 71 below in math. Students with disabilities were chronically absent at about 18% (compared with 12% for all students) and suspended at about 4% (compared with 2%).

37-73791.what_this_means_for_parents: About 39% of autistic students in this district are in a general-education classroom for most of the day, with around 3% in a fully separate setting, so many spend part of the day in specialized settings. 37-75416.overview: Warner Unified is a small, rural K-12 district in the North Inland region of San Diego County. About 413 of its students have an IEP, and 70 of them — roughly 17% — have an autism (ASD) eligibility. Around 76% of those autistic students spend most of the school day in a general-education classroom.
37-75416.what_this_means_for_parents: About 76% of autistic students here are in a general-education classroom for most of the day, and none are reported in a fully separate setting.
